page: Amazon Web Services m9gd.xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an arm64-based AWS Graviton processor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores using the ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 architecture running at 2.8 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The processor is equipped with 64 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 MB L3 cache. The system is configured with 16.0 GB of RAM, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of a 237 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. Baseline network bandwidth is rated at 2 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m9gd.xlarge demonstrates strong memory and compression capabilities. The PassMark memory score and single-threaded gzip compression at 36.53 GB/sec both rank in the top tier (top 10%), alongside single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 17.27 GB/sec. However, raw CPU performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core metrics falling into the average category, and the overall PassMark CPU score ranking in the bottom 25%. Large-block memory write bandwidth is measured at 41.51 GB/sec, while static web serving throughput reaches an average tier of 14.86 GB/sec.

From a resource density perspective, the inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage alongside dedicated ARMv9 cores offers a balanced hardware profile for general-purpose workloads. The high memory performance and top-tier compression speeds make this instance highly efficient for data processing, caching, and web serving. It is well-suited for workloads such as Redis key-value stores, static web serving, and lightweight database operations, though it is less optimal for heavy CPU-bound encryption tasks or large-scale LLM prompt processing.
description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by a 4-core ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 processor running at 2.8 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 16.0 GB of RAM, a 237 GB local NVMe SSD instance store, and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The arm64 architecture delivers top-tier performance in memory benchmarks and single-threaded compression tasks, such as gzip and bzip3, while maintaining average performance in multi-core processing and web serving. The dedicated CPU cores and integrated local storage provide a balanced resource profile without the need for external storage attachments. This instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, including caching layers like Redis, static web serving, and lightweight database operations that benefit from high memory bandwidth and local SSD storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.xlarge is a general-purpose arm64 instance featuring 4 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V3 cores, 16 GB RAM, and a 237 GB local NVMe SSD, optimized for high-speed memory and compression workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose instance with 4 Graviton cores, 16 GB RAM, and 237 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An arm64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores, high-speed local NVMe SSD storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth performance.
bullet_points: Powered by a 4-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 ARMv9 processor running at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM and a 237 GB local NVMe SSD instance store
Delivers top-tier performance in memory bandwidth and single-threaded compression benchmarks
Features a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and dedicated Nitro hypervisor allocation
Optimized for caching, static web serving, and lightweight database workloads
